purpose: Canonical, reusable column contract for the Operations Follow-up & Task Backbone (docs/OPERATIONS_FOLLOWUP_BACKBONE.md). Any SharePoint list that opts into follow-up reminders + calendar/Planner sync adopts THIS set. Defined once here; the CRM is the first consumer. Column definitions are mirrored into the consuming list's schema config (e.g. crm.sharepoint.json) and applied by the existing gated apply path. When a second consumer is onboarded, generalise the apply to read this file directly (noted as future hardening in the backbone doc).
namingNote: Backbone columns are namespaced with the 'op_' internal-name prefix so they never collide across the many lists that will adopt them. Display names stay clean and human.

mappedFields.note: These two roles are NOT new columns. Each consuming list maps them to its own existing date + owner columns via config/followup.registry.json (fieldMap). The CRM maps due -> FollowUpDueDate and owner -> ItemOwner.

due.role: The follow-up date/time. The ONLY field that syncs two-way (calendar/Planner <-> hub).

owner.role: Person who owns the follow-up. Drives Planner assignment + calendar attendee, so it lands on their surfaces.

columns.purpose: Which email alerts the owner wants for this item, relative to the due date/time. Optional, opt-in. Offsets are relative, so the engine is timezone-independent.

Where this follow-up should also be reflected. Calendar = an Outlook event (owner as attendee, lands on their calendar). Planner = a task in the function's plan, assigned to the owner. Opt-in per item.
Foreign key to the Outlook event the engine created for this item. Hidden technical field. Enables two-way date sync without duplicates.
Foreign key to the Planner task the engine created for this item. Hidden technical field.
The due value the engine last propagated everywhere (the sync 'shadow'). The loop-breaker: the engine only writes when a system's current value differs from this. Hidden technical field.
Last sync result / conflict resolution / error, for observability. Hidden technical field.

safetyEnvelope: Adopting this contract on a list only ADDS columns (additive, non-destructive). The hidden technical columns must be ShowInNewForm=false AND ShowInEditForm=false (same rule as crm.intake.json.blockedFieldNames). No deletes, no permission/sharing/consent changes.
